name: I think my coughing and shortness of breath is from chemicals at work in Murray, what do I do first?

mainEntity.name: I think my coughing and shortness of breath is from chemicals at work in Murray, what do I do first?

acceptedAnswer.text: Seek medical care now, report to employer in writing, and document everything. File Utah workers' comp claim quickly for coverage[1][2].
acceptedAnswer.description: **Immediately seek medical attention** and report the symptoms to your employer in writing, requesting air quality testing under OSHA standards. In Utah, workplace respiratory exposure typically falls under workers' compensation, covering medical care and lost wages if proven work-related[1][2]. Employers must evaluate hazards when there's potential overexposure, per Ninth Circuit ruling on OSHA 1910.134[1]. - Get a doctor's note linking symptoms to work exposure. - Document your job duties, exposure duration, and any safety gear provided. - File a workers' comp claim promptly to preserve benefits. Save all records and avoid signing anything without review; consult a workers' comp attorney in Murray for free evaluation to protect your claim.
